$comment: THE single source of framework metadata for Flutter/Material (ADR-18). Generated into BOTH language domains — the analyzer's extraction adapter and the compiler's widget registry. Neither domain hand-writes any of this. A fact stated twice is a fact that will eventually be stated two different ways, and we have already paid for that twice: canonical numbers (100.0 vs 100) and this catalog (a flat slot set in Dart, a per-widget catalog in TypeScript).

componentPropsGetter.$comment: The getter a `State` uses to reach its `StatefulWidget`'s fields. `widget.isDark` is a read of the *component's* parameter `isDark` — extraction already lifts those fields onto `ui.Component.params`, so the read is a parameter reference and `widget` is the framework's word for 'my own props' (INV-22). Without this the receiver reached the generator as an undeclared name and every component that reads a prop was refused with BRG3006. flutter/widgets.dart:State.widget.
componentPropsGetter.name: widget

changeNotificationCalls.$comment: Framework calls that announce a change and carry no other meaning (INV-22). ADR-4/ADR-20: *a signal write IS the notification*, so the announcement is already implied by the write the UIR records — and the name is one no downstream pass may know. `notifyListeners()` in a ChangeNotifier action is erased, exactly as `setState`'s wrapper is spliced open. Erased rather than modelled: a `sig.Action` that wrote nothing would still announce nothing, so there is no information here to keep. flutter/foundation.dart:ChangeNotifier.

navigation.$comment: Navigator 1 vocabulary (Spec v2.4 §A17). `pushRoute` takes a Route OBJECT whose destination is built inline -> RouteTransition.component. `pushPath` takes a PATH string -> RouteTransition.target. `pop` is not an edge at all: it returns along one that already exists. `settingsNameProp` is the property an onGenerateRoute switch selects on — reading that switch is what makes a router written as a function visible to the static route graph (M7-C/M6-D: the corpus declares zero `routes:` maps and routes entirely through onGenerateRoute).

lazyBuilders.$comment: Constructors whose children are produced by an `itemBuilder` closure over an index range rather than written as a list. Extraction expands them into `ui.List` — the same node `for (x in xs) W(x)` and `xs.map((x) => W(x)).toList()` produce — so nothing downstream has to know a builder existed. Keyed `Widget.constructor`. ADR-18: the fact that `ListView.builder`'s closure is called `itemBuilder` and its count `itemCount` is framework metadata, and belongs here rather than in an extractor.

curves.$comment: Flutter's easing curves, from animation/curves.dart (Flutter 3.44.0). Every entry below is declared in the SDK as `Cubic(x1, y1, x2, y2)` — which is exactly CSS's `cubic-bezier(x1, y1, x2, y2)`, the same four control points of the same unit cubic Bezier. This is a correspondence, not an approximation, which is why the implicit-animation family can be implemented without an animation engine. `linear` is `_Linear` (curves.dart:1468) and maps to the CSS keyword. The curves NOT here have no CSS equivalent and are refused by name: `decelerate` (_DecelerateCurve, :1477), the ThreePointCubics `fastEaseInToSlowEaseOut` (:1500) and `easeInOutCubicEmphasized` (:1772) — two joined cubics, which one `cubic-bezier()` cannot express — and the bounce and elastic families (:1869-1884), which are piecewise and spring curves respectively.

rebuildBuilders.$comment: Widgets whose only purpose is to SCOPE A REBUILD. Flutter needs them because `setState` rebuilds a whole `State`; a `ListenableBuilder` exists so that only its subtree rebuilds when a listenable fires, and a `Builder` exists only to obtain a fresh `BuildContext`. Neither is a fact about the user interface. INV-22 is explicit: 'no framework lifecycle or runtime primitive may survive extraction… their meaning is already carried by UIR constructs.' Under ADR-4 and ADR-20 a signal read IS the subscription, so the rebuild scope a Flutter program had to state by hand is what the signal graph computes — and a `BuildContext` has no UIR meaning at all. So extraction replaces these with their builder's body. `valueProp` names the parameter whose listenable binds the builder's *value* parameter; the builder's `context` and `child` parameters are framework machinery and bind to nothing. Together these are the corpus's 2nd and 3rd most-used unsupported widgets — 41 instantiations.

widgets.$comment: Not `role: text`. That role collapses a widget into a `ui.Text` node, which carries no record of which widget produced it — so a SelectableText became indistinguishable from a Text and lost the one thing that makes it a different widget. It is an ordinary element whose positional argument the catalog names, exactly as Icon's is.
widgets.name: SelectableText

widgets.$comment: The reason this migration exists. `slivers` is an ordered child list under a name that is not `children` — and extraction, which hardcoded the literal string `children`, buried it in props as an opaque expression. CustomScrollView is the backbone of every scrolling Flutter screen.
widgets.name: CustomScrollView

widgets.$comment: `actions` is an ordered child list. One buried in hello_bridge's own props.
widgets.name: AppBar

widgets.$comment: Positional argument names differ per constructor, which is why positionalProps is keyed by constructor: Flutter declares Image.asset(String name) and Image.network(String src). The unnamed constructor takes `image:` as a named ImageProvider and has no positional argument, so it declares none.
widgets.name: Image

widgets.$comment: `.extended` is a factory on the SAME class, so `icon` and `label` are parameters of FloatingActionButton itself. Extraction keys on the type, not the constructor — so the entry carries the union. A separate `FloatingActionButton.extended` entry was inert, and N8's verification is what found it.
widgets.name: FloatingActionButton
ElevatedButton
widgets.$comment: `.icon` is a factory on the SAME class, so `icon` and `label` are parameters of it — found by M5-A running a real application, which reported BRG2112 for `TextButton.icon`.

widgets.name: TextButton
widgets.$comment: `.icon` is a factory on the SAME class, so `icon` and `label` are parameters of it — found by M5-A running a real application, which reported BRG2112 for `TextButton.icon`.

widgets.name: OutlinedButton
widgets.$comment: `.icon` is a factory on the SAME class, so `icon` and `label` are parameters of it — found by M5-A running a real application, which reported BRG2112 for `TextButton.icon`.

Wrappers whose identity IS their behaviour: no `transparentWithoutProps`, so they are never flattened. A `Center` with no props still centres. Guessing that a prop-less widget must be a pass-through is how a compiler silently deletes a layout.
widgets.name: Center

widgets.$comment: `decoration` is a value (InputDecoration), not a slot — its own prefixIcon/suffixIcon are widgets, and they are slots *of the decoration*, which UIR carries inside the constructed value rather than on the field.
widgets.name: TextField

widgets.$comment: Wrappers that CAN be transparent — a pass-through carrying none of the props that make it significant.
widgets.name: Container

widgets.$comment: A rendering hint with no layout or semantic effect. Every target repaints on its own terms, so it never survives to one — transparent with no conditions at all.
widgets.name: RepaintBoundary

widgets.$comment: `children` holds the DrawerHeader and the destinations, in order.
widgets.name: NavigationDrawer

widgets.$comment: M3's bottom navigation. Its ordered child list is `destinations`, not `children`.
widgets.name: NavigationBar

widgets.$comment: `label` is a String prop here, not a slot — unlike NavigationRailDestination's, which is a Widget. The two spellings are Flutter's, not ours.
widgets.name: NavigationDestination

widgets.$comment: `NavigationRailDestination` is NOT a Flutter Widget, and naming `destinations` here is what puts the rail's items into the UI tree anyway: extraction asks the catalog for the children property first and only falls back to inferring it from the type. Without this entry the whole list landed in `props` as an expression and N8 reported BRG2110 — whose message blamed the frontend. It was the catalog that was missing. See docs/m4/m4g-shells-and-navigation.md §3.
widgets.name: NavigationRail

widgets.$comment: M2's bottom navigation, superseded by NavigationBar. `BottomNavigationBarItem` is not a Widget either — the same catalog mechanism carries it.
widgets.name: BottomNavigationBar

widgets.$comment: Sizes what it wraps for a slot that demands a preferred size — Scaffold's `appBar`, AppBar's `bottom`.
widgets.name: PreferredSize

widgets.$comment: An in-tree banner. Not `Banner`, which is the debug ribbon and is not something an application authors.
widgets.name: MaterialBanner

widgets.$comment: Present so its structure survives extraction and the generator can refuse it *by name* with the reason. A SnackBar is shown by a call, not by being in the tree.
widgets.name: SnackBar

widgets.$comment: The implicit-animation family: a target value and a duration, which is a CSS transition. Not the explicit family, which needs a per-frame value.
widgets.name: AnimatedOpacity

widgets.$comment: Present so its structure survives extraction and the generator refuses it by name: an exit transition needs the outgoing child to outlive its replacement, which React does not do.
widgets.name: AnimatedSwitcher

widgets.$comment: Its `children` are the expanded body; `title`/`subtitle`/`leading`/`trailing` are the header.
widgets.name: ExpansionTile

widgets.$comment: `ButtonSegment` is NOT a Widget — the same shape as NavigationRailDestination. Naming `segments` as the children property is what puts them in the UI tree; see docs/m4/m4g §3.
widgets.name: SegmentedButton

widgets.$comment: Present so its structure survives extraction and the generator refuses it by name: reordering is a drag, which is the gesture model.
widgets.name: ReorderableListView

widgets.$comment: Present for the refusal. `columns` and `rows` are two ordered widget-bearing lists on one element, and `ui.Element` holds one — see the M4-I report §5.
widgets.name: DataTable

widgets.$comment: Present so its structure survives extraction and the generator refuses it by name: the cross-fade from placeholder to loaded image is the exit transition `AnimatedSwitcher` is refused for, applied to a load event the kit does not observe.
widgets.name: FadeInImage

widgets.$comment: `.icon` is a factory on the SAME class, so `icon` and `label` are parameters of it. Extraction keys on the type, not the constructor — the FloatingActionButton precedent.
widgets.name: FilledButton

constValues.$comment: A static const of one of these types is extracted as its VALUE, not as a reference. `Icons.star` is `IconData(0xe5f9, fontFamily: 'MaterialIcons')`; the codepoint is the icon's identity and the Dart name is an alias for it. Referencing it by name would oblige every runtime kit to carry Flutter's ~2000-entry Icons table. The listed fields are read off the evaluated constant and become the named arguments of a synthesised construction.

material.$comment: Material's own numbers, transcribed from the Flutter SDK at the version in `flutterVersion`. Every value cites the file and line it came from. INV-20 forbids literals in kit components; this is where they live instead, and the kit reads them from generated source. A value that cannot be cited does not belong here.

stateLayerOpacity.$comment: material/elevated_button.dart:550-556 (_ElevatedButtonDefaultsM3.overlayColor). `dragged` is deliberately absent: WidgetState.dragged exists in Flutter but no M3 defaults class assigns it an opacity, so there is no framework value to state.

elevationOverlay.$comment: material/elevation_overlay.dart:172-177 (_surfaceTintElevationOpacities). M3 renders elevation as a surface tint. Interpolated linearly between stops and clamped outside the range, per _surfaceTintOpacityForElevation at :45-67.

icon.$comment: widgets/icon_theme_data.dart:51-59 (IconThemeData.fallback). fontFamily from material/icons.dart, which declares every IconData with it.

components.$comment: Per-component M3 defaults. `colorRole` names a MaterialRole the theme resolves — never a literal colour (INV-20).

AppBar.$comment: material/app_bar.dart:2521-2557 (_AppBarDefaultsM3) — elevation 0, scrolledUnderElevation 3, toolbarHeight 64, background surface, foreground onSurface, leading icon onSurface, actions icon onSurfaceVariant, shadow and surfaceTint transparent. titleSpacing is NavigationToolbar.kMiddleSpacing = 16 (widgets/navigation_toolbar.dart:39). Note toolbarHeight 64 is the M3 default; kToolbarHeight (constants.dart:30) is 56 and is M2.

Badge.$comment: material/badge.dart:_BadgeDefaultsM3 — smallSize :494, largeSize :495, padding EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal:4) :496, alignment AlignmentDirectional.topEnd :497, background error :505, text onError :508. The offset is computed in build (:228-234), is direction-dependent, and carries an unconditional +Offset(0,8) shim, so it is not a single value to state.

BottomNavigationBar.$comment: material/bottom_navigation_bar.dart:933-936 — the selected colour is brightness-dependent: colorScheme.primary in light, colorScheme.secondary in dark. Height kBottomNavigationBarHeight = 56 (material/constants.dart:33). DIVERGENCE, stated as Tooltip's is: the SDK's unselected colour is `themeData.unselectedWidgetColor`, which theme_data.dart:484 resolves to the literals Colors.black54 / Colors.white70. A kit component may hold no literal (INV-20), so the catalog names M3's onSurfaceVariant — the role those literals stand in for — instead of inventing a hex.

BottomSheet.$comment: material/bottom_sheet.dart:1483-1505 (_BottomSheetDefaultsM3) — elevation 1, a 28px top corner radius, maxWidth 640, background surfaceContainerLow, drag handle onSurfaceVariant. The drag handle size is Size(32, 4) (:137-138, :155).

Card.$comment: material/card.dart:_CardDefaultsM3 — elevation 1.0, margin EdgeInsets.all(4.0), colour surfaceContainerLow, shape RoundedRectangleBorder(12.0).

Checkbox.$comment: material/checkbox.dart — Checkbox.width = 18.0 (:405), _kStrokeWidth = 2.0 (:651), side width 2.0 in onSurfaceVariant at rest (_CheckboxDefaultsM3 :33). The selected fill is primary and the check is onPrimary.

Chip.$comment: material/chip.dart:_ChipDefaultsM3 — label onSurfaceVariant :2510-2519, side BorderSide(outlineVariant) :2539, iconTheme primary at size 18 :2544-2549, padding EdgeInsets.all(8) :2552, height _kChipHeight :36. NOTE: _ChipDefaultsM3.color returns null with the comment 'Subclasses override this getter' (:2516) — a bare Chip has NO background default in the SDK, so none is stated here and the component paints no fill.

ChoiceChip.$comment: material/choice_chip.dart:272-330 (_ChoiceChipDefaultsM3) — an 8px rounded rectangle (not a stadium, unlike a bare Chip), selected fill secondaryContainer with onSecondaryContainer label, labelLarge. Geometry (height, padding) is the Chip entry's, which this reuses rather than restating.

CircleAvatar.$comment: material/circle_avatar.dart — _defaultRadius 20.0 :177 (diameter 40.0), M3 background primaryContainer :209-210, foreground onPrimaryContainer :203-204. There is no _CircleAvatarDefaultsM3 class; these are private statics and inline M3 branches.

CircularProgressIndicator.$comment: material/progress_indicator.dart:_CircularProgressIndicatorDefaultsM3Year2023 (:1535) — again the EFFECTIVE class, since year2023 defaults true (:1130). Diameter 36.0 from constraints :1552 (the 2024 class's 40.0 does not apply), strokeWidth :1546, strokeAlign center = 0.0 :1549, colour primary :1543.

Drawer.$comment: material/drawer.dart:61 (_kWidth 304), :778-806 (_DrawerDefaultsM3) — elevation 1, background surfaceContainerLow, shadow and surfaceTint transparent, and a 16px rounded end edge the SDK notes has no token yet but is in the spec.

ExpansionTile.$comment: material/expansion_tile.dart:907-925 (_ExpansionTileDefaultsM3) — text onSurface both states; icon primary when expanded and onSurfaceVariant when collapsed. The tile reuses ListTile's geometry, so its heights are read from the ListTile entry rather than restated here.

FloatingActionButton.$comment: material/floating_action_button.dart:775-833 (_FABDefaultsM3) — elevation 6, 56x56 regular / 40x40 small / 96x96 large, extended height 56 with 8px icon-label spacing, background primaryContainer, foreground onPrimaryContainer, icon 24 (36 large), radius 16 regular / 12 small / 28 large. Scaffold insets it by kFloatingActionButtonMargin = 16 (material/floating_action_button_location.dart:25).

IconButton.$comment: material/icon_button.dart:995-1012 (the documented _IconButtonDefaultsM3 style) — padding all(8), minimumSize 40x40, iconSize 24, StadiumBorder, elevation 0, transparent background (:1070-1071), foreground onSurfaceVariant (:1074-1083).

InputDecorator.$comment: material/input_decorator.dart:_InputDecoratorDefaultsM3 — activeIndicatorBorder is onSurfaceVariant at rest (:56), primary at width 2.0 when focused (:51), error (:48), and onSurface at 0.38 when disabled (:39). Outline disabled is onSurface at 0.12 (:62). contentPadding for the outline border is EdgeInsets.fromLTRB(12, 16, 12, 8) (:3341). The floating label scales by _kFinalLabelScale (:41).

LinearProgressIndicator.$comment: material/progress_indicator.dart:_LinearProgressIndicatorDefaultsM3Year2023 (:1555). This is the EFFECTIVE class: year2023 resolves to true by default in 3.44.0 (:595-601), so the 2024 class's borderRadius of 2.0 does NOT apply and the default is null — hence no borderRadius here. minHeight :1568, colour primary :1562, track secondaryContainer :1565. Indeterminate cycle _kIndeterminateLinearDuration = 1800ms (:23).

ListTile.$comment: material/list_tile.dart:_LisTileDefaultsM3 (the SDK's own typo). contentPadding is EdgeInsetsDirectional.only(start:16,end:24) at :1829; minLeadingWidth :1830; minVerticalPadding :1831. horizontalTitleGap is not in the defaults class — it is the literal 16 at :1029. Heights come from _defaultTileHeight at :1507-1509 (non-dense).

MaterialBanner.$comment: material/banner.dart:501-519 (_BannerDefaultsM3) — elevation 1, background surfaceContainerLow, divider outlineVariant, content bodyMedium. Padding from :350-357 — start 16, top 24, end 16, bottom 4 for the two-line form; leading padding end 16 (:211).

NavigationBar.$comment: material/navigation_bar.dart:1427-1480 (_NavigationBarDefaultsM3) — height 80, elevation 3, background surfaceContainer, indicator secondaryContainer with a StadiumBorder sized 64x32 (:29-30), icon size 24, icon/label onSurfaceVariant unselected and onSecondaryContainer selected, labelMedium.

NavigationDrawer.$comment: material/navigation_drawer.dart:726-750 (_NavigationDrawerDefaultsM3) — elevation 1, tileHeight 56, indicatorSize 336x56, background surfaceContainerLow, indicator secondaryContainer, icon/label onSurfaceVariant unselected and onSecondaryContainer selected.

NavigationRail.$comment: material/navigation_rail.dart:1233-1272 (_NavigationRailDefaultsM3) — elevation 0, minWidth 80, minExtendedWidth 256, background surface, indicator secondaryContainer, icon size 24, unselected icon onSurfaceVariant and selected onSecondaryContainer, labels labelMedium in onSurface. Indicator geometry from :24-25 — _kCircularIndicatorDiameter 56, _kIndicatorHeight 32.

SegmentedButton.$comment: material/segmented_button.dart:_SegmentedButtonDefaultsM3 — a stadium-ended row of outlined segments; selected segment fills secondaryContainer with onSecondaryContainer content, unselected onSurface, outline for the border, labelLarge, 40 high.

Slider.$comment: material/slider.dart:_SliderDefaultsM3Year2023 — the EFFECTIVE class, since year2023 defaults true as it does for the progress indicators. trackHeight 4.0 (:super), activeTrackColor primary, inactiveTrackColor surfaceContainerHighest, thumbColor primary. The 2024 class's trackHeight is 16.0 and does not apply.

Switch.$comment: material/switch.dart draws the M3 switch with a painter rather than from size constants, so no width/height is transcribable — the geometry below is the kit's own, stated as such. The COLOUR roles are Material's: a selected track is primary with an onPrimary thumb, an unselected one surfaceContainerHighest with an outline thumb and an outline track border.

TabBar.$comment: material/tabs.dart:2795-2830 (_TabsPrimaryDefaultsM3) — indicator primary, selected label primary, unselected onSurfaceVariant, both titleSmall, divider outlineVariant at height 1. Tab height _kTabHeight = 46 (:30); a tab with both text and icon is 72 (:31).

ToggleButtons.$comment: material/toggle_buttons.dart:449 — `borderRadius` is themeable with no M3 defaults class; M3 supersedes ToggleButtons with SegmentedButton. DIVERGENCE, stated as Tooltip's and BottomNavigationBar's are: the catalog names SegmentedButton's own M3 roles so the two render consistently, rather than inventing a palette for a component the SDK leaves to the M2 theme.

Tooltip.$comment: material/tooltip.dart — verticalOffset :389, showDuration 1500ms :392, waitDuration Duration.zero :394, borderRadius Radius.circular(4) :496/:511, height 32.0 and padding symmetric(16,4) on the touch platforms :428/:439. DIVERGENCE, stated rather than hidden: Flutter 3.44 paints a tooltip with LITERAL colours (Colors.white / Colors.grey[700] at :495/:510), which INV-20 forbids a kit component from holding. The roles below are Material 3's own plain-tooltip roles, and using them is a deliberate, documented departure from this Flutter version's implementation rather than an invention — a literal here would be the compiler bug INV-20 names.
